edges.target: Altered distal calcium and magnesium handling

edges.description: Renal salt wasting keeps blood pressure low to normal despite RAAS activation.
Chronic renal salt wasting and volume contraction are associated with salt craving.
intermediate_mechanisms: Chronic sodium chloride wasting and compensatory salt appetite.

edges.description: Chronic renal salt wasting is associated with nocturia and polyuria-polydipsia symptoms.
Collecting-duct hydrogen ion wasting produces metabolic alkalosis.
Potassium and magnesium depletion contribute to muscle weakness.
intermediate_mechanisms: Chronic potassium and magnesium depletion affecting neuromuscular excitability.

edges.description: Electrolyte depletion contributes to cramps and spasms.
intermediate_mechanisms: Chronic potassium and magnesium depletion affecting neuromuscular excitability.

edges.description: Potassium and magnesium depletion can prolong ventricular repolarization.
intermediate_mechanisms: Hypokalemia and hypomagnesemia altering cardiac repolarization.

edges.description: Altered distal magnesium handling produces hypomagnesemia.
edges.source: Altered distal calcium and magnesium handling

edges.description: Chronic magnesium depletion is associated with adult chondrocalcinosis.
edges.source: Altered distal calcium and magnesium handling

edges.target: NCC loss in distal convoluted tubule

nodes.description: NCC loss in the distal convoluted tubule alters distal divalent cation handling, producing the diagnostic combination of hypomagnesemia and low urinary calcium excretion. The exact molecular coupling remains incompletely resolved, but distal convoluted tubule calcium-magnesium crosstalk and altered TRPV5/6 and related transport pathways are implicated.
nodes.id: Altered distal calcium and magnesium handling

evidence.reference_title: Renal calcium and magnesium handling in Gitelman syndrome.
evidence.snippet: GS patients classically present with hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis with hypocalciuria and hypomagnesemia.

evidence.reference_title: Renal calcium and magnesium handling in Gitelman syndrome.
evidence.snippet: the mechanisms by which hypomagnesemia and hypocalciuria develop in GS are poorly understood

nodes.description: Adult patients can develop chondrocalcinosis, likely related to chronic magnesium depletion.

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0000934 | Chondrocalcinosis | Very rare (<4-1%)

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Characteristics, Symptoms, and Long-Term Outcomes in Gitelman Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Adult patients with GS had a high prevalence of chondrocalcinosis (15%)

nodes.description: Salt wasting and aldosterone activation increase distal sodium delivery and collecting-duct sodium reabsorption, promoting potassium and hydrogen ion secretion. This produces hypokalemia and metabolic alkalosis and contributes to cramps, weakness, paralysis risk, and ventricular repolarization changes.

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman syndrome: an analysis of the underlying pathophysiologic mechanisms of acid-base and electrolyte abnormalities.
evidence.snippet: The syndrome is usually characterized by hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis in combination with hypomagnesemia and hypocalciuria.

evidence.reference_title: Generation and analysis of the thiazide-sensitive Na+ -Cl- cotransporter (Ncc/Slc12a3) Ser707X knockin mouse as a model of Gitelman syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Expression of epithelial Na(+) channel (Enac), Ca(2+) channels (Trpv5 and Trpv6), and K(+) channels (Romk1 and maxi-K) were significantly increased.

nodes.description: Reduced distal sodium chloride reabsorption causes chronic renal salt wasting and extracellular volume contraction. Blood pressure remains low to normal because the tubular defect prevents effective sodium retention.

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman syndrome: consensus and guidance from a Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO) Controversies Conference.
evidence.snippet: Gitelman syndrome (GS) is a rare, salt-losing tubulopathy characterized by hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis with hypomagnesemia and hypocalciuria.

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman syndrome: pathophysiological and clinical aspects.
evidence.snippet: hypokalemia, hypomagnesemia, hypocalciuria, metabolic alkalosis and hypereninemic hyperaldosteronism

nodes.description: Low circulating potassium is the dominant electrolyte abnormality and a driver of weakness, cramps, paralysis risk, and electrocardiographic repolarization abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002900 | Hypokalemia | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman syndrome: an analysis of the underlying pathophysiologic mechanisms of acid-base and electrolyte abnormalities.
evidence.snippet: hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis in combination with hypomagnesemia and hypocalciuria

nodes.description: Low circulating magnesium is common and contributes to neuromuscular and cardiac manifestations; lower blood magnesium was associated with greater symptom burden in a 2025 survey.

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002917 | Hypomagnesemia | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Characteristics, Symptoms, and Long-Term Outcomes in Gitelman Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Symptom burden was higher in adult females and patients with lower blood magnesium.

nodes.description: Chronic salt wasting typically produces normal or low blood pressure despite renin and aldosterone activation.

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002632 | Low-to-normal blood pressure | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Characteristics, Symptoms, and Long-Term Outcomes in Gitelman Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Compared with the general population, adult patients with GS had lower rates of chronic kidney disease (CKD) and hypertension

nodes.description: Oral magnesium supplementation targets hypomagnesemia and may also help stabilize potassium by improving magnesium-dependent potassium handling.

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman syndrome: consensus and guidance from a Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO) Controversies Conference.
evidence.snippet: oral magnesium and potassium supplements

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Characteristics, Symptoms, and Long-Term Outcomes in Gitelman Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Treatment mainly consisted of potassium (94%) and magnesium (50%) supplementation.

nodes.description: Metabolic alkalosis follows from increased distal hydrogen ion secretion in the setting of volume contraction, aldosterone activation, and potassium wasting.

evidence.snippet: HP:0200114 | Metabolic alkalosis | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.snippet: hypokalemic metabolic alkalosis with hypomagnesemia and hypocalciuria

nodes.description: Cramps and spasms are common neuromuscular consequences of chronic potassium and magnesium depletion.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003394 | Muscle spasm | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.snippet: Physicians reported muscle cramps, salt craving, and muscle weakness as most common GS symptoms.

nodes.description: Muscle weakness reflects chronic or episodic potassium and magnesium depletion and is among the most commonly reported symptoms.

nodes.description: SLC12A3 loss-of-function variants reduce NCC activity in distal convoluted tubule epithelial cells, decreasing thiazide-sensitive sodium chloride transport across the apical membrane.

evidence.snippet: mutations of the SLC12A3 gene that encodes the thiazide-sensitive sodium-chloride cotransporter in the early distal convoluted tubules

evidence.reference_title: Renal calcium and magnesium handling in Gitelman syndrome.
evidence.snippet: This gene encodes the thiazide-sensitive sodium-chloride cotransporter (NCC) which is exclusively expressed in the distal convoluted tubules (DCT).

evidence.reference_title: Transcriptional and functional analyses of SLC12A3 mutations: new clues for the pathogenesis of Gitelman syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Heterologous expression documented a novel class of NCC mutants with defective intrinsic transport activity.

nodes.description: Nocturia and polyuria/polydipsia can occur as part of chronic salt-wasting tubulopathy.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000017 | Nocturia | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman Syndrome: A Rare Cause of Seizure Disorder and a Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: It usually presents in late childhood or in teenage as nonspecific weakness, fatigability, polyuria, and polydipsia

nodes.description: Oral potassium chloride supplementation is used to counter chronic potassium wasting and reduce hypokalemia-related symptoms and arrhythmia risk.

nodes.description: Potassium-sparing agents such as amiloride, spironolactone, or eplerenone can be used when supplementation alone does not adequately improve serum potassium or symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Characteristics, Symptoms, and Long-Term Outcomes in Gitelman Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Potassium-sparing medication (used in 33%) slightly increased blood potassium levels (3.2 vs. 3.1 mmol/l).

evidence.reference_title: Gitelman's variant of Bartter's syndrome, inherited hypokalaemic alkalosis, is caused by mutations in the thiazide-sensitive Na-Cl cotransporter.
evidence.snippet: identify a wide variety of non-conservative mutations, consistent with loss of function alleles, in affected subjects

evidence.reference_title: Clinical Characteristics, Symptoms, and Long-Term Outcomes in Gitelman Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Data from 587 patients (25% pediatric) across 13 countries showed 93% were genotyped, with 94% having variants in SLC12A3.

nodes.description: Salt craving reflects chronic renal salt wasting and is among the symptoms patients rated as most severe in the 2025 survey.

nodes.description: Extracellular volume contraction stimulates renin secretion and secondary aldosterone activation, which increases collecting-duct sodium reabsorption and drives potassium and hydrogen ion wasting.
